page: Microsoft Azure E2ns_v6 is an Env6-family, network-optimized server instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. This configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The instance is designated as network-optimized, prioritizing network throughput over local disk resources.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated as average, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve average marks. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and memory bandwidth (both small and large block reads/writes) fall into the poor category. Passmark CPU, memory, and database operations scores are weak, as is the static web serving throughput of 3.73 GB/sec. Redis SET operations perform poorly at 2.35 million operations per second. For lightweight machine learning, prompt processing for a 135M LLM model is weak, though text generation for the same model reaches an average speed of 87.7 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ns_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for a 2-vCPU footprint, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense multi-core parallel processing or high local storage IOPS. Given the benchmark results, this server is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic network-optimized routing, single-threaded data processing, and small-scale web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw multi-threaded compute power.
description: The Microsoft Azure E2ns_v6 is a network-optimized, x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It provides 2 vCPUs from a single physical core and is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it contains no GPUs. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, contrasted by poor multi-core performance and weak memory bandwidth. Passmark database and memory scores are weak, and Redis SET operations are poor. Qualitatively, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for entry-level deployments. It is best suited for memory-sensitive, single-threaded workloads, lightweight database tasks, and network-oriented services that do not demand high multi-core compute density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ns_v6 is a network-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 16 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering average single-core but weak multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2ns_v6 features 2 Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, suitable for memory-sensitive, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized, dual-vCPU instance featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-sensitive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Network-optimized configuration with Premium Storage capability and no local storage
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weak multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
Best suited for lightweight database operations and memory-sensitive, single-threaded tasks
